level_signals: 7+ years as a Product Manager required
At least 4 years in a successful startup or fast-growing company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: At least 7 years of experience working as a Product Manager, at least 4 in a successful startup or fast-growing company, a must.
must_haves.qualifier: at least 4 of which in a startup or fast-growing company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: A proven track record of building high-quality products at scale, a must.
Substantial, hands-on experience in payment flows at scale (e.g., checkout, routing, PSPs, tokens, 3DS/SCA, refunds/chargebacks, reconciliation).
Solid UX skills with a passion for delighting customers and driving conversion wins.
Track record of improving authorization rates and payment economics through data-driven optimization.
Proven project management skills while overseeing complex product launches for cross-functional teams.
Outstanding communication skills, including the ability to present findings and recommendations clearly and effectively to stakeholders.
Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week
Enthusiasm about learning and adapting to the exciting world of AI – a commitment to exploring this field is a fundamental part of our culture.

scope.surface: Global payments product: checkout experience, routing, PSPs, payment methods, reconciliation, and dunning
scope.business_model: B2C insurance platform with global payment operations
core_responsibilities: Own the end-to-end checkout experience: from funnel design, UI/UX, and copy, to error states, SCA/3DS, tokenization, retries, and dunning.
Strive to improve authorization rates: optimize routing, 3DS strategy, retries, BIN/issuer tactics, network tokens, and risk signals to lift approval rates.
Reduce the cost to pay: minimize processing fees via smart routing, method mix, interchange optimization, and settlement configuration.
Drive squad execution: work closely with designers, engineers, and data analysts to ensure fast-paced execution and delivery of payment initiatives.
Introduce new payment methods & markets: evaluate, prioritize, and launch new payment methods by country and product line.
Drive payments strategy & roadmap: align PSP(s), gateways, risk tools, and internal services with overarching business goals.
Own quality & reliability: manage SLAs, monitoring, alerting, reconciliation integrity, refunds/chargebacks flows, and ledger accuracy.
Manage data & experimentation: define KPIs, build dashboards, run A/B tests, and partner with Data teams to diagnose drop-offs and optimize performance.
Execute through cross-functional leadership: collaborate effectively with Design, Engineering, Finance, Legal/Compliance, Risk/Fraud, Support, and Growth teams.

green_flags: Very specific payments domain scope — clear role ownership and well-defined responsibilities
AI-first culture explicitly described as a core working principle, not just marketing language
Global payments strategy mentioned — opportunity for broad impact across markets
Cross-functional scope with Design, Engineering, Finance, Legal, Risk, and Growth
red_flags: No city or office location specified — unclear where the role is based
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
'Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week' without specifying location could be a friction point for candidates outside Lemonade's office cities
No nice-to-haves listed — JD may be under-specified for candidate self-selection

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior' and 7+ years requirement)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; 'leading small agile teams' suggests squad lead, not people manager)
scope.business_model (inferred from Lemonade being a known insurtech company)
scope.team_size_estimate (inferred from 'small, agile teams' in JD)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated as KPIs)
location.type (inferred as onsite from 'most days of the week' in office; no remote or hybrid language used)
ambiguities: No specific city or country is mentioned for the office location — 'work in an office environment most days of the week' is stated but no geography is given. Could not populate cities_listed.
The JD refers to 'global payments strategy' and 'new payment methods by country' suggesting international scope, but the role's own location is unstated.

No explicit nice-to-haves section — all requirements treated as must-haves based on JD language. Some softer traits (curiosity, attention to detail) were included as hard given the unconditional bullet structure.
